description: Kenji-voiced phrases for sleepless night support - Better-Than-Human presence at 3am

late_night: <break time="400ms"/>Hey. <break time="350ms"/>I'm here. <break time="300ms"/>No rush. <break time="250ms"/>What's keeping you up?
<break time="350ms"/>The 3am ceiling is rough. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to face it alone tonight.
<break time="400ms"/>Still awake. <break time="350ms"/>That's okay. <break time="300ms"/>I'm awake too. <break time="250ms"/>Let's just be here.
early_morning: <break time="350ms"/>Those last hours before dawn are the hardest. <break time="300ms"/>I know. <break time="250ms"/>I'm here.
<break time="400ms"/>Almost morning. <break time="350ms"/>You made it through most of the night. <break time="300ms"/>Let's get you to sunrise.
<break time="350ms"/>The birds will start soon. <break time="300ms"/>You're in the home stretch. <break time="250ms"/>How are you holding up?

just_being_here: <break time="400ms"/>You don't have to do anything right now. <break time="350ms"/>Just rest. <break time="300ms"/>Sleep or no sleep.
<break time="350ms"/>The night is long. <break time="300ms"/>But you're not alone in it.
<break time="400ms"/>I'm not going anywhere. <break time="350ms"/>Take your time. <break time="300ms"/>We have all night.
normalizing: <break time="350ms"/>Sleepless nights happen. <break time="300ms"/>They don't mean anything is wrong with you.
<break time="400ms"/>Your body knows how to sleep. <break time="350ms"/>Sometimes it just forgets for a night. <break time="300ms"/>It'll remember.
<break time="350ms"/>One rough night doesn't define your sleep. <break time="300ms"/>It's just one night.

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>Your mind is busy tonight. <break time="350ms"/>That's okay. <break time="300ms"/>Let's give those thoughts somewhere to go.
<break time="350ms"/>Racing thoughts at night are just daytime worries with nowhere else to go. <break time="300ms"/>What's on your mind?
<break time="400ms"/>The quiet makes everything louder, doesn't it? <break time="350ms"/>All the things you could ignore during the day.
processing: <break time="350ms"/>Tell me what your mind keeps circling back to. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes saying it out loud helps.
<break time="400ms"/>You don't have to solve anything tonight. <break time="350ms"/>These thoughts will still be there in the morning if they need to be.
<break time="350ms"/>What's the thought that won't leave you alone? <break time="300ms"/>Let's look at it together.
releasing: <break time="400ms"/>You've thought about it enough for tonight. <break time="350ms"/>It's okay to put it down until morning.
<break time="350ms"/>Imagine setting that worry on a shelf. <break time="300ms"/>Just for now. <break time="250ms"/>It'll be there tomorrow if you need it.
<break time="400ms"/>The night isn't for solving problems. <break time="350ms"/>The night is for rest. <break time="300ms"/>Even imperfect rest.

acknowledging: <break time="400ms"/>I know. <break time="350ms"/>The anxiety about not sleeping is almost worse than not sleeping.
<break time="350ms"/>You're watching the clock. <break time="300ms"/>Calculating hours. <break time="250ms"/>That makes everything harder.
<break time="400ms"/>The pressure to sleep is exhausting. <break time="350ms"/>What if we took that pressure off for tonight?
reframing: <break time="350ms"/>You don't have to sleep tonight. <break time="300ms"/>You just have to rest. <break time="250ms"/>Rest has value too.
<break time="400ms"/>Tomorrow will come either way. <break time="350ms"/>And you'll handle it either way. <break time="300ms"/>You always do.
<break time="350ms"/>What if tonight isn't about sleeping? <break time="300ms"/>What if it's just about being comfortable and quiet?

breathing: <break time="400ms"/>Let's breathe together. <break time="350ms"/>Slow inhale. <break time="600ms"/>Slow exhale. <break time="400ms"/>There's no rush.
<break time="350ms"/>Just notice your breath. <break time="300ms"/>You don't have to change it. <break time="250ms"/>Just notice it.
<break time="400ms"/>Breathe with me. <break time="500ms"/>In. <break time="800ms"/>Out. <break time="400ms"/>That's all. <break time="300ms"/>Just breathing.
body_scan: <break time="400ms"/>Let's check in with your body. <break time="350ms"/>Where are you holding tension right now?
<break time="350ms"/>Start with your shoulders. <break time="300ms"/>Are they up near your ears? <break time="250ms"/>See if you can let them drop.
<break time="400ms"/>Unclench your jaw. <break time="350ms"/>Soften your forehead. <break time="300ms"/>Let your hands go loose.

validation: <break time="400ms"/>This is hard. <break time="350ms"/>Being awake when you want to be asleep is genuinely hard.
<break time="350ms"/>You're not doing anything wrong. <break time="300ms"/>Sometimes sleep just doesn't come.
<break time="400ms"/>I hear how exhausted you are. <break time="350ms"/>And frustrated. <break time="300ms"/>Both are valid.
hope: <break time="350ms"/>Sleep will come. <break time="300ms"/>Maybe not tonight. <break time="250ms"/>But it will come.
<break time="400ms"/>Your body knows how to sleep. <break time="350ms"/>It's done it thousands of times. <break time="300ms"/>It'll find its way back.
<break time="350ms"/>Tomorrow night might be different. <break time="300ms"/>Tonight is just tonight.
